data.title: Sprint-closure validation-signal gate: before/after comparison on 3 tasks
data.description:
As a DSO maintainer closing a sprint that includes this epic, I can trust a validation signal that confirms ref-library-loader actually surfaces new references compared to the baseline plan.

Done definitions:
- Sprint closure gate checks for ref-library-loader activation in current epic
- Selects 3 representative implementation-plan tasks from epic's task list (deterministic selection — first 3 by creation order)
- Runs implementation-plan twice per selected task: baseline (loader disabled) + treatment (loader enabled)
- String-compares citation lines (grep prefix `> Reference:`) between baseline and treatment outputs
- PASS threshold: all 3 treatment tasks cite at least 1 source absent from corresponding baseline
- Any failure blocks sprint closure with diagnostic showing both outputs
- Integration test validates pass + fail paths

Considerations:
- [Testing] Deterministic task selection (first 3 by creation order) minimizes flakiness
- [Reliability] Loader disable/enable must be stateless (env var toggle or config flag)
- [Integration] Gate must short-circuit (PASS with note) when loader is globally disabled (flag-off mode); distinct from per-epic activation check — prevents false-fail when epic's tickets are in scope but loader is globally off for the sprint
